sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना । अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥
hindiTranslation: कई मनुष्य ध्यानयोगके द्वारा, कई सांख्ययोगके द्वारा और कई कर्मयोगके द्वारा अपनेआपसे अपनेआपमें परमात्मतत्त्वका अनुभव करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Some realize the true self within through meditation on the self. Others through clear discernment and yoga, and still others through the yoga of action.
essence: The same realization opens through different disciplines.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: People realize the true self in different ways: through meditation, through clear discernment, or through the yoga of action.
simpleInsight: You do not need one perfect doorway. Different temperaments can reach the same realization.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna explains that the true self can be known in more than one way: through meditation, through discernment, or through action done as yoga. The teaching widens the path.
modernRelevance: You sit in a quiet room, trying to force clarity before a hard decision, and nothing comes. This verse reminds you that insight may arrive through stillness, analysis, or steady action.
translationLiteral: ध्यानेन (through meditation) / आत्मनि (in the self) / पश्यन्ति (they see) / केचित् (some) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / आत्मना (by the self) / अन्ये (others) / सांख्येन (through discernment) / योगेन (through yoga) / कर्मयोगेन (through the yoga of action) / च (and) / अपरे (others)
